parsed_review.summary: CP00-772 (RP25.P05.M06.S09-S18, risk A, 10 units, all "Synthetic Fixture Set") is a descriptor-only Migration Platform pack and passes hardened read-only review. Baseline consistency: the deliverable distribution {implementation:3, security_audit:2, fixture:1, test:3, hermes_evidence:1} and unit titles match across prompt, manifest plan_binding_snapshot, registry MIGRATION_PLATFORM_CP772_REQUIREMENTS (FIXTURE_ROWS.slice(8,18)), and review baseline. Implementation: service.js:1170-1225 adds the CP772 descriptor/case-set (section_count 1)/Hermes+Claude packets/CP00-772->CP00-773 handoff through shared deep-frozen helpers that embed MIGRATION_PLATFORM_NO_WRITE_ATTESTATION; index.js re-exports via export *. Validation logic: validators.js:1442-1512 enforces descriptor_only rows and blocks source-system runtime opening (file_server/sharepoint/drive/imanage/import), cross-tenant access, raw/source/credential/real-client payloads, unverified knowledge creation, Claude-final-approval, and local-validation enterprise-trust claims. Tests/commands: model.test.js:621-674 adds 3 passing CP772 tests and the contract validator includes the cp772 entry; command-evidence.json records all 10 required validation commands plus npm test and git diff --check at exit_code 0 (no non-zero codes), and the regenerated contract has validation.valid:true with latest_projection MigrationPlatformCp772SyntheticFixtureTailDescriptor and no "valid":false anywhere. Boundaries: Hermes is used only for deterministic descriptor evidence (emits_runtime_receipt:false, writes_product_state:false); Claude remains read-only and non-final (review-request read_only:true, tools Read/Grep/Glob; descriptor authority_boundary claude_is_final_approval:false, human_final_approval_required:true). The manifest honestly reports production_ready:false / runtime_ready:false with no runtime or enterprise-trust over-claim, and adjudication/construction-inspection correctly sit in PENDING_REVIEW awaiting this verdict (expected closeout sequence). Scope hygiene verified: unrelated dirty files (progress-control-room, workbook/*, the v2.0.docx, and docs/ldip-integration/.../c-ldip-01-invalid-attempt-01.json from a different workstream) are correctly excluded; active_untracked_implementation_files_omitted is empty; the cosmetic double-listing of the pack's own docs/closeout-packs/cp00-772/ dir in unrelated_dirty_files_preserved is a systemic scope-file convention also present at the already-closed CP00-771, and the authoritative manifest worktree_transition_policy/commit_policy classifies pack scope correctly. Review-evidence hardening: readiness auth-preflight passed with valid JSON (exit 0, 1445-byte stdout) and there are no recorded invalid/malformed prior review attempts to reject. No P0/P1/P2/P3 findings; this PASS is the signal to finalize adjudication/inspection and flip the descriptor-verified gate. Final human approval for any future runtime/enterprise-trust opening remains outside this receipt, as expected.
